creator: luizgustavo@luizgustavo.pro.br
text:
Em Sex, 2012-05-11 Ã s 22:27 +0400, Ruslan Mahmatkhanov escreveu:

> Hi Luiz,
> 
> This port has two major issues:
> 
> - it depends on textproc/py-whoosh that we have not in the ports tree
> - port is missing pkg-plist file, so it can be deinstalled correctly
> 

For a rapid reply, I created a port for this depend:
http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=167185

> for the first issue - looks like py-whoosh need be ported first before 
> we can ever deal with rhodecode port.
> for the second issue you may read this section of Porters Handbook [1]
> 
> I also suggest this patch [3]. What it does:
> - some space/tab twiddle to make it look better (non-functional changes)
> - rephrase COMMENT
> - USE_PYTHON was defined twice
> - it's more common to list OPTIONS as multiline knob, so I removed that
>    +='s
> - including bsd.port.pre.mk for options handling is deprecated way to
>    do this. Use bsd.port.options.mk instead. See there [2].
> 
> Please let me know what you think about this and if you need any help.
> 
> 1. 
> http://www.freebsd.org/doc/en_US.ISO8859-1/books/porters-handbook/porting-desc.html#AEN105
> 2. 
> http://www.freebsd.org/doc/en_US.ISO8859-1/books/porters-handbook/makefile-options.html#AEN2601
> 3. http://people.freebsd.org/~rm/rhodecode.diff
> 
> 

Nice, that's right, I'm still learning how to do port, this is my second
port :) (my first is: www/luakit)

Thank you for the help


--
